APPEARANCES: JESSICA ALEXANDRA, ESQUIRE (BEHREND MORRISON & ALEXANDRA, 415.956.0549) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JOHN P. MCDONNELL, ESQUIRE (MYERS HAWLEY MOBLEY MYERS & MCDONNELL, 650.948.1600) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-25-2008 Continued To Feb-04-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARIE DUGGAN, JOHN DUGGAN, ORIGINAL JOE'S, INC. AND CAROL ROHAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 4, 2008. THE COURT REQUIRES THAT THE MOVING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE ANOTHER SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS. WHEN SUBMITTING THE DUPLICATE COPIES, PLEASE PROVIDE A COVER LETTER TO THE COURT REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE SO THAT THE COURTESY COPIES WON'T BE MISPLACED WHEN RECEIVED. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Moton To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Demand For Production Of Documents, And For Award Of Monetary Sancions;
Off Calendar Jan-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff David Sullivan's motion to compel production of documents is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to requests #13 and #14. The motion is granted as to the remaining requests but limited to the period of July 1, 2004 to January 1, 2007 for the third and fourth floor of the subject property. If any documents are withheld pursuant to a claim of either the attorney client or attorney work product privilege, they shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

Notice And Motion To Compel Documents Production In Compliance With Deposition Notice
Off Calendar Nov-01-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
John P. McDonnell, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Marie Duggan
Jessica Alexandra, Esq., and Paul Behrend, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Ian Jensen
HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Marie Duggan's motion to compel production of documents is granted. Plaintiffs are ordered to produce the specified documents within 5 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that plaintiffs David Sullivan and Jeremy Forbing failed to respond to an authorized method of discovery. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $925 are to be paid by plaintiffs within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2007 Set For Trial On Mar-03-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Feb-13-08 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 503

Notice And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Aug-29-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED. Defendant Marie Duggan and John Duggan's motion for a protective order is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to the form interrogatories. The motion is granted as to the special interrogatories and requests for production of documents without prejudice to plaintiff propounding more specific discovery after counsel for the parties have met and conferred. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
